title: Why too Many Gods? | BG 7.20 - 7.23 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Tanya Cushman Reviewer:" Peter van de Ven
So today we'll be discussing from the seventh chapter of the Gita,
where Krishna talks about the Devatas.
And the topic for today's discussion is, why too many gods?
Especially in our religion, we call ourselves as Hindus,
we have how many gods and goddesses?
33 crore Devi and Devatas.
So sometimes we wonder, what is all this mess?
Everyone is worshipping different gods, different rituals, different superstitious beliefs,
at least when we look at Christianity, we see at least they have some common understanding.
Islam, they talk about Prophet Muhammad.
We have somebody worshipping Shiva, we have somebody worshipping Hanuman,
we have somebody worshipping Durga, somebody worshipping Kali.
And then some people say, you have to have something to worship, some form to worship.
These are all means. Whatever suits you, whatever suits you.
Whatever suits you, whatever is resonating with you, you can take that help.
Ultimately, God is formless.
It's a means to an end. The ultimate end is impersonal.
So today, we'll get clarity on this particular subject.
We are covering verses 20 to 23 from the seventh chapter,
based on the teachings of His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishala Prabhupada.
Let's chant this verse together.
Sorry, 20th verse.
Please repeat after me.
kama istertir hrithik jnanah
prapadyante anyadevatah
tam tam niyamam asthaya
prakritaya niyata svaya
Translation.
Those whose intelligence has been stolen by material desires,
I'll say by myself.
Those whose intelligence has been stolen by material desires, surrender unto demigods
and follow the particular rules and regulations of worship according to their own natures.
Lord N declarant ho
Krishna Narayanam
Lord Ram
Lord Narasimha
Lord Ganesha
various avatars of the Lord fall under the category of Vishnu Tattva, Tattva
means category and there's another category called Jeeva Tattva, we fall in
which category Vishnu Tattva or Jeeva Tattva, Jeeva Tattva and even in Jeeva we
have example in this world, somebody is very empowered, he has lot of power, lot of following,
lot of fame, lot of wealth and there are some so called ordinary Jeevas also, you see lot
of gradation, the power which the President of United States wields compared to an ordinary
man, phenomenal difference.
So likewise there are several categories.
There are some empowered entities, living entities or Jeevas who have been given the
position of Deva or Devatas.
So Devatas also are Jeeva Tattva category, Brahma, Indra, all different Devatas are of
the Jeeva Tattva category but they are not ordinary Jeevas.
There are some empowered entities, living entities or Jeevas who have been given the
position of Deva or Devatas.
Because they are devotees of the Lord and because they have been empowered by the Lord
they are made in charge of specific aspects of Universal Affairs.
For example Indra is in charge of the reins.
Now, the next question is –can God not manage all these departments of Universal Affairs
on this own?
Just like in an organisation, you need the managers, different verticals heading towards
command.
Yes, you need the races.
by different HODs because the boss cannot manage. It's not possible practically. Whereas
we have the understanding that God is supreme, He is all-powerful. Then why does He need
different Devatas to manage different universal affairs? Can anyone tell me with this answer?
Why does Lord need different Devatas to manage different departmentals of universal affairs?
Yeah. Okay. So there are living entities, Jivas, who are devotees of the Lord but still
they have a desire to control, to Lord, to enjoy. And they are given different positions.
Still, they are not Akama Bhaktas. Akama Bhaktas means free from all desires. So Devatas
are empowered living entities. Just like you have the Prime Minister of the country. It's
a position. It's not a person. Before Narendra Modi, Manmohan Singh was the Prime Minister.
And that position was occupied by Narendra Modi. And after him, somebody else will also
occupy. Exactly like this, different departmental affairs are controlled by different Jivas,
empowered to that position. Now, who worships different Devatas? Now, Devatas are in charge
of different departmental affairs. As I mentioned, this Govardhan Puja we celebrated, everyone
was worshipping Indra. Why they were worshipping Indra? Because they want rains. Why? Because
they want rains. Indra is in charge of rains. And if we please him, then we'll have enough
of rainfalls. And in that past time, Krishna tells the Vrajavasis, no need of worshipping
Indra. You worship Govardhan. And that infuriated Indra. He became angry. We saw the video,
some of you were there for the festival. Because sometimes when you are elevated to
a strong, powerful position, you can become proud. So we have many, many examples of sometimes
when pride overpowers a person's intelligence, and a person loses sense of what is the source
of that power. It can happen to any one of us.
But those whose minds are distorted by material desires, they want some material benedictions
or blessings. They worship different Devatas. There was one person who used to very enthusiastically
organize Kali Puja. And you know with a lot of grandeur, big festival, a lot of goats
were sacrificed. And it was a very big affair. A lot of people were invited and . . . you
big sacrifices used to happen and as this person became old slowly the
grandeur of the festival started declining so one day a person came to
him and he said over the years we have seen that the festival has so to say
decreased in size and the grandeur and the number of goats getting sacrificed
why is it so he says don't you see my teeth have gone now I have become old in
other words many of these sacrifices or so-called worship is done for some
selfish desires to meet some selfish needs Vedic literatures
recommend that people who have sacrificed their life for the sake of
worship of different gods for different purposes knowing that people are not
ready for the ultimate thing we have come in this material world rebellious
rebelled against the Lord with a lot of desires
each are Dresha Samudena from that state to come back to the original state where
we are conscious of the Supreme Lord free from
material desires have become pure cowardice of the Lord it's a big journey
now in this state where we're completely forgetful of not we think we are the
Lord we think via the control of we think we are the Enjoyer the Lord has
said that okay if you want material desires to be fulfilled here are
different empowered representatives now this kind of worship is overallσε
all in one sense a business transaction. Different worship, different rituals, different sacrifices
happen. Ultimately, even they may be worshipping, doing some puja, it's all for some material
gain. In our temple lives too, I think mentioned earlier, in Bangalore we have a very big temple.
Invariably before the exams, we see a lot of students visiting the temple. Before elections,
we see a lot of politicians visiting the temple. We can really, seeing the crowd, we
can make out what season it is. So for material gain, for some material benefit, those who
are not devotees of the Lord think that for certain purposes, Devatas are much better
than Supreme Lord. At least they can give something. In fact, there's one person said
that, what is the use of worshipping Tulsi Maharani? It gives nothing. Better worship
a Brinjal.
At least it gives Brinjal. You see the understanding? Tulsi doesn't give you any vegetable, doesn't
give you anything. Why worship Tulsi? Better worship a vegetable. Because the basic underlying
principle is, what will I get in return? It is far, far away from genuine spirituality.
Text 21.
I am in everyone's heart.
As Supersoul. Who is saying these words? Krishna to Arjuna. I am in everyone's heart as the
Supersoul, as Paramatma. As soon as one desires to worship some demigod, I make his faith
steady so that he can devote himself to that particular deity. If suppose we are attached
to a particular deity or we are very keen on worshipping a particular Devata, who makes
that faith steady? Krishna is saying, I make that faith steady. Krishna is saying, I make
that faith steady. Yes, yes, worship, worship. Better than not worshipping anything and
thinking that I am all in all, I am all powerful, I don't need to depend on anyone. It's better,
it's a very big step in spiritual evolution to think that I need to have blessings of
someone. There is some higher authority on whom we have to depend on. Although it may
not be highest authority, but still, etc.
That is a very big step in spiritual evolution to understand that I need somebody's help.
I need somebody's blessings. So Krishna is saying, as soon as one desires to worship
some demigod, I make his faith steady so that he can devote himself to that particular deity.
Now, Lord has given us independence, has given us free will. When we desire to worship the
material things lord will not interfere this question comes up many times if lord is all
powerful then why can't he force us to do good things why can't he force us to desire the right
things all the problem will be solved the answer is the lord has given us a free will
independence and he does not want to interfere in that because if he forces us to act in a
particular way then how are we different from sofia you understand what is sofia these days
we have that robot that robots so how are we different it's just programmed to act in a
particular way so the lord does not interfere when we have some material desires and we are

very
keen that my desires get fulfilled the lord leaves it although he knows that it is not in

surrender to me
now the faith when we worship a particular devata the faith is given by the lord
and then for
there's another verse which will come to now which says the resultant benediction that
the sub that the devata gives is also given by the supreme law
the faith the inspiration to worship that devata is also given by the lord
and the resultant benediction the blessings what we get from the devata
that also is given by the supreme lord krishna is saying because devatas are his
amshas a man of love and Holy Spirit and the resultant benediction that the deivata gives
Any way you are worshipping me only, but it is avidhipurvakam.
Vidhi means proper method, proper procedure, avidhipurvakam means it's not the right way.
Just like when you offer water to a plant, you put the water on the leaf, water on the flower.
You are watering the plant only, but that's not the right way.
If you put the water on the root, automatically the entire plant, all the twigs, all the branches
and all the leaves gets nourished.
Both the demigods and living entities are dependent on the supreme will for their faith
and for the resultant blessings.
In ignorance people believe that each and every demigod is a separate god and a competitor
of the supreme lord.
We think that all these are separate gods.
My god is better than your god or I have more faith in my god.
Yes.
The answer is there is how many gods?
One god.
There is no Muslim god, Christian god, Hindu god and there are no 33 crore gods.
There is only one god.
What is Vishnu Tattva?
Vishnu Tattva is Keshavadhrita Narahari Rupa Jai Jagadishaare.
For various pastimes, for various leelas, when Prahlad Maharaj was questioned, is your
lord there in this form?
He said yes.
When Trinakashyapu had the benediction, I should not die in the day or night on land
or on air.
To fulfill all the benedictions which Trinakashyapu got, lord, the same Keshava, the same Vishnu,
the same Krishna took the form of Narasimha.
Vishnu, Narayana, Narasimha, Rama, Balarama, all these are lord himself in his various
forms.
There are Leela Avatara, there is Shakti Aveshavataras, there is Yuga Avatara.
They all fall under the category of Vishnu Tattva.
And then there are different Devatas.
Now these different Devatas, without proper knowledge, we think that they all are independent
gods, which is not the case.
Text 22.
knows how to water the plant, he'll put the water only on the root. Devotional service
to the Supreme Lord and the worship of a demigod are not on the same platform. Some people
think that we can worship anything and it's all the same. You like worshipping so and
so, you worship. I like worshipping this thing. I feel more comfortable worshipping so and
so devata. We think that it is all the same. It's all about lighting some agarbatti, doing
some pooja. Any pooja you do, it's all the same. Actually, it is not. Devotional service
to the Lord, pure devotion to the Lord, pure bhakti and worship of different devatas, they
are completely different commodity altogether. The results achieved by demigods' benedictions
are perishable because they themselves are jivas. They cannot bestow us freedom from
this cycle.
This cycle of birth and death. They cannot give us mukti. They can give only material
benedictions and these material benedictions are temporary. There is an element of selfishness
in such worship. Once there was a worshipper of Kali and you know he worshipped Kali and
wanted some benediction in return. He did lot of pooja and finally Kali got pleased
with him and gave that benediction. In return, he had promised that I will offer a goat.
So deal was done and finally when he got the benediction, Kali reminded him. Goddess Kali
reminded him that you are supposed to offer a goat. What about that goat? So he told this
demonetization has happened and recently GST is rolled out. Goats have become very
costly. GST 18% has got. So it's difficult now. So the Kali said okay if it's difficult
then okay why don't you offer a pig then? It's relatively cheaper. So then this person
you know after few days when he comes back when he was reminded to offer a pig he says
actually these days it's very difficult to find pigs. You don't see pigs around. It's
very difficult.
So the Goddess said see I have given the benediction. We had a deal. At least you should fulfill
this commitment what you had made. Okay if you cannot offer a pig at least offer a small
fly. At least I will get property. At least something you have given me in return. So
this person says why are you asking me flies? There are so many flies buzzing around you.
Why don't you take one of them? Why you have to ask me?
There is an element of selfishness and this story is a very famous one which I have I
think told n number of times. A person worships Lord for a lottery ticket. First prize is
one lakh rupees and he is in a very impoverished condition and worships and worships and says
Lord somehow please give me the first prize. First prize is for one lakh rupees and if
I get the prize we will do 50 50.
50,000 yours 50,000 mine. So the day lottery result was declared. Unfortunately instead
of the first prize he got the second prize. Instead of one lakh rupees he got 50,000 rupees
as a prize. He goes to God and says you are very smart you have already kept your share
50 percent.
to God and He says you're very smart you've already kept your share 50 percent and given
me only my share and he kept the entire 50,000 for him. Because the intent is material desires,
there is element of selfishness, we do business with God. By devotional service of the Lord
one achieves, now in contrast to worship of Devatas, the devotional service entails
somehow to please the Supreme Lord and that is not a means to an end, that's an end in itself.
Whether you embrace me or you make me broken hearted by not being present before me,
you are my Lord unconditionally. Pure devotion is unconditional.
Now in case of normal scenario, when we are not devotees of the Lord, the Lord is witnesser
and sanctioner. Based on our karma we get what is due to us. But when we become devotees of the Lord,
when we start surrendering to the Lord, which is not a binary logic, 0 or 1,
then the Lord is not just the witnesser and sanctioner.
He also becomes a director….
He starts involving in our lives, directing us
I am there in everyone's heart, from me comes knowledge,
remembrance and forgetfulness…
So, devotee of the Lord is always thinking,
how do I can please the Lord, how I can act in a way,
is pleasing to the lord and the the lord in turn is serving his devotee which is exemplified in
the case of arjuna and krishna the lord has agreed to become a chariot driver of arjuna partha sarti

that spiritual world is beyond this material world which is manifest and unmanifest

within material world just like somebody can go to u.s it's much more prosperous

the standard of living may be much higher but still it is material world so worship of devatas

So first thing is, does the benediction when we worship a particular God gets fulfilled?
The answer is yes. Here in this verse also we are talking about, Krishna says when one
worships a particular Devatas and he gets his benediction fulfilled, it is coming from
me only. But the results are temporal.
It is not the highest thing which we can be asking. Again story, we have shared this
earlier. There was a lady working, she was very impoverished, very old and her son had
deserted her and she was working as a laborer in a construction company, in the hot sun
would be carrying bricks to the construction site and she was quite devoted.
And every day, you know, in the hot sun she would be carrying the bricks and at the end
of the day she would get some part to some which can manage her living. So one day while
she was carrying, somehow she stumbled and all the bricks fell. And you know, because
she was devoted, says Govinda, Govinda, please help me. So Govinda actually came seeing that
a devoted lady. So she says, what do you want? What do you want? He says, I need your help
to carry the bricks so that I can carry it strongly. I don't fumble. I don't stumble.
Can you please help me give the strength so that I can carry the bricks nicely? What's
wrong in this ask? Can anyone tell me? The Lord himself has come. And the lady is saying,
can you please help me with the strength so that I can carry the bricks better? What's
wrong in this asking? What's wrong in this ask?
She didn't have the intelligence.
There are much higher things. The Lord can give much more. The Lord can give much more.
But we are asking some very petty thing. So if we have the right understanding, the right
knowledge, we can ask the right things.
Why so much fear? The base of everything that we would desire.
Fear, I think a lot of thing emerges from ignorance. A lot of superstitious beliefs
emerge from ignorance. And because you are ignorant, there is an element of fear. Kya
ho jayega? And then there are all kind of superstitions. Yes, Deepakji?
What is wrong with the part of the lady who asks only for what occurs to her? If the Lord
is so magnanimous, he can also give much more than what is being asked.
As we discussed in previous slide, Lord being magnanimous to everyone actually can give
us without even we asking it. Just like a father to his son. Not necessarily the son
always asks. The father is always ready to do something for the betterment of the child.
Much more than what he is asking for. But specifically when it comes to this thing,
we have come with the intent that I am asking for. I am asking for this. I am asking for
this. I am asking for this. I am asking for this. I am asking for this. I am asking for
this. I am asking for this. I am asking for this.
I don't want you. Leave me alone. I will make my independent plans for enjoyment.
In other words, although we may not explicitly say, we have said, we have said like this,
I don't want you. I have my own independent plans of enjoyment. Don't disturb me. That's
the base state why we have come into this material world.
Ichadw esadha samuthena. So, Lord has given us a free will. He does not intervene. You
want this material. God has given you this, and he says I want it. So you sit, you are
material desires, okay, take it. You don't want me, okay, take it. You want to forgive
me, take it. Although that's not the highest thing. The Lord knows that a person will unnecessarily
suffer. But that's what we want. That's why Krishna says, bahu naam janma naam ante, after
many many lives of trial and error, this way, that way, this way, that way, gyan vaan maam
prapadyanti, a person in knowledge will come to me. Vasudevam sarvam iti, that Vasudeva
is everything. I have to approach him, I have to surrender to him. Samahatma durlaba, that
atma is a real person. We don't want him, that's why, why he should force upon us the
highest thing. We still want material desires to be fulfilled. We are not interested in
anything spiritual. We are not interested in surrendering to him. We are not interested
in obeying his commands. We are not interested in obeying his commands. We are not interested
in obeying his instructions. We are interested in my desires getting fulfilled by hook or
crook. You tell me that way, I'll do it. So, Lord doesn't want to thrust upon anything
us, among us.
